samples.xml_description:
<module id="traffic_light">
    <description>Implement a traffic light with red, yellow, green indicators and a pedestrian button. In normal conditions, the motor vehicle lane light should be green for 60 clock cycles, yellow for 5 clock cycles, and red for 10 clock cycles. When the pedestrian button is pressed, if the remaining green time is greater than 10 clocks, it is shortened to 10 clocks. If it is less than 10 clocks, it remains unchanged. Only the lane light is considered in this task.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="pass_request">Pedestrian button input.</input>
        <output id="clock" width_description="[7:0]">An 8-bit output representing the count value of the internal counter.</output>
        <output id="red">Motor vehicle lane red signal output.</output>
        <output id="yellow">Motor vehicle lane yellow signal output.</output>
        <output id="green">Motor vehicle lane green signal output.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="idle">Idle state.</parameter>
        <parameter id="s1_red">State 1 - Red signal.</parameter>
        <parameter id="s2_yellow">State 2 - Yellow signal.</parameter>
        <parameter id="s3_green">State 3 - Green signal.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic>
            <description>State transition logic.</description>
            <assign>
                <data>state</data>
                <value>
                    <switch>
                        <case condition="state == idle">idle</case>
                        <case condition="state == s1_red">s1_red</case>
                        <case condition="state == s2_yellow">s2_yellow</case>
                        <case condition="state == s3_green">s3_green</case>
                    </switch>
                </value>
            </assign>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic>
            <description>Output logic.</description>
            <assign>
                <data>red</data>
                <value>
                    <switch>
                        <case condition="state == s1_red">1'b1</case>
                        <case condition="state == s2_yellow">1'b0</case>
                        <case condition="state == s3_green">1'b0</case>
                    </switch>
                </value>
            </assign>
            <assign>
                <data>yellow</data>
                <value>
                    <switch>
                        <case condition="state == s1_red">1'b0</case>
                        <case condition="state == s2_yellow">1'b1</case>
                        <case condition="state == s3_green">1'b0</case>
                    </switch>
                </value>
            </assign>
            <assign>
                <data>green</data>
                <value>
                    <switch>
                        <case condition="state == s1_red">1'b0</case>
                        <case condition="state == s2_yellow">1'b0</case>
                        <case condition="state == s3_green">1'b1</case>
                    </switch>
                </value>
            </assign>
        </sequential_logic>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <description>
            The traffic light controller operates in three states: idle, red, yellow, and green. The controller uses a counter to manage timing for each state. The counter is reset to 10 when the reset signal is active low. The controller transitions between states based on the counter value and the pedestrian request. The output signals (red, yellow, green) are set based on the current state. The controller operates as follows:
            1. In the idle state, the controller transitions to the red state and sets the red signal to 1.
            2. In the red state, the controller waits for a count of 3. If the count is reached, it transitions to the green state and sets the green signal to 1. If not, it remains in the red state.
            3. In the green state, the controller waits for a count of 3. If the count is reached, it transitions to the yellow state and sets the yellow signal to 1. If not, it remains in the green state.
            4. In the yellow state, the controller waits for a count of 3. If the count is reached, it transitions back to the red state and sets the red signal to 1. If not, it remains in the yellow state.
            The controller also responds to the pedestrian request. If the pedestrian request is received and the green signal is active, the controller shortens the green state to 10 clocks. If the green signal is inactive and the previous green signal was active, the controller extends the green state to 60 clocks. If the yellow signal is inactive and the previous yellow signal was active, the controller sets the counter to 5 clocks. If the red signal is inactive and the previous red signal was active, the controller sets the counter to 10 clocks.
        </description>
    </implementation>
</module>
